---
title: "Accélérateurs (GPU/TPU/LPU/NPU)"
date: today
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD"
---

# Accélérateurs pour IA

## NVIDIA — CUDA Ecosystem

### Hopper (H100/H200)

| Caractéristique | H100 SXM5 | H100 PCIe | H200 SXM5 |
|----------------|-----------|-----------|-----------|
| Architecture | Hopper | Hopper | Hopper |
| Mémoire | 80 GB HBM3 | 80 GB HBM3 | 141 GB HBM3e |
| Bande passante | 3.35 TB/s | 2 TB/s | 4.8 TB/s |
| TDP | 700W | 350W | 700W |
| FP8 | 3,958 TFLOPS | 1,979 TFLOPS | ~3,958 TFLOPS |
| Interconnect | NVLink 4 (900 GB/s) | PCIe 5 | NVLink 4 (900 GB/s) |
| Contexte LLM | ~70B model (FP8) | ~70B quantized | ~120B model (FP8) |

### Blackwell (B200/B300)

| Caractéristique | B200 | B300 (attendu) |
|----------------|------|----------------|
| Architecture | Blackwell | Blackwell Ultra |
| Mémoire | 192 GB HBM3e | 288 GB HBM3e |
| Bande passante | 8 TB/s | ~10 TB/s |
| TDP | 1000W | ~1200W |
| FP4 | 18,000 TFLOPS | ~25,000 TFLOPS |
| Interconnect | NVLink 5 (1.8 TB/s) | NVLink 5 |

::: callout-warning
## Disponibilité Blackwell
Les B200 sont en production limitée depuis Q4 2024. La demande dépasse largement l'offre. Les clusters B200 sont réservés aux hyperscalers et partenaires prioritaires.
:::

### GeForce (Consumer/Pro)

| GPU | Mémoire | Architecture | Cas d'usage |
|-----|---------|--------------|-------------|
| RTX 4090 | 24 GB GDDR6X | Ada Lovelace | Dev local, fine-tuning léger |
| RTX 5090 | 32 GB GDDR7 | Blackwell | Dev local, inference 70B quantized |
| RTX 6000 Ada | 48 GB GDDR6 | Ada Lovelace | Workstation, multi-model |

## AMD — ROCm Ecosystem

### Instinct MI300

| Caractéristique | MI300X | MI325X | MI350 (attendu) |
|----------------|--------|--------|-----------------|
| Architecture | CDNA3 | CDNA3 | CDNA4 |
| Mémoire | 192 GB HBM3 | 288 GB HBM3e | 288 GB HBM3e |
| Bande passante | 5.3 TB/s | 8 TB/s | ~8 TB/s |
| TDP | 750W | 1000W | ~1000W |
| FP8 | 10,475 TFLOPS | 13,094 TFLOPS | ~15,000 TFLOPS |

::: callout-note
## ROCm maturity
ROCm 6.x a considérablement amélioré la compatibilité avec PyTorch et les frameworks LLM (vLLM, SGLang). L'écart avec CUDA se réduit, mais l'écosystème reste moins mature.
:::

## Google — TPU

| TPU | Mémoire | Bande passante | Disponibilité |
|-----|---------|----------------|---------------|
| v5e | 16 GB HBM | 0.82 TB/s | GCP (Vertex AI) |
| v5p | 95 GB HBM | 2.76 TB/s | GCP (Cloud TPU) |
| Trillium (v6e) | 32 GB HBM | 1.6 TB/s | GCP (2025) |
| Ironwood (v7) | 192 GB HBM | 7.4 TB/s | GCP (2026 annoncé) |

## Groq — LPU

| Caractéristique | GroqChip | GroqRack |
|----------------|----------|----------|
| Mémoire | 230 MB SRAM (par chip) | — |
| Bande passante | 80 TB/s (par chip) | — |
| Latence | <1 ms (first token) | — |
| Débit | 500+ tokens/s (Llama 70B) | — |
| Architecture | Tensor Streaming Processor | 256 chips/rack |

::: callout-tip
## Groq : ultra-low latency
Groq excelle en inférence temps réel (latence sub-ms). Limité par la SRAM (pas de HBM), idéal pour les modèles tenant en cache. Pricing au token compétitif.
:::

## Edge & NPU

| Accélérateur | Fabricant | TOPS | Cas d'usage |
|-------------|-----------|------|-------------|
| M4 Neural Engine | Apple | 38 TOPS | On-device inference (MLX) |
| M4 Max | Apple | 38 TOPS + GPU 40-core | Dev local, inference 70B |
| Hexagon NPU | Qualcomm | 45 TOPS | Mobile, Snapdragon X Elite |
| Core Ultra NPU | Intel | ~48 TOPS | Laptop, OpenVINO |
| Ryzen AI NPU | AMD | 50 TOPS | Laptop, RyzenAI |
| Jetson Orin | NVIDIA | 275 TOPS | Robotics, edge AI |

## Critères de choix

```{mermaid}
graph TD
    Q[Quel est votre cas d'usage?] --> TRAIN{Training?}
    TRAIN -->|Oui| SCALE{Échelle?}
    SCALE -->|Massif| H100B200[H100/B200 cluster]
    SCALE -->|Moyen| A100[H100/A100 multi-GPU]
    SCALE -->|Petit| RTX[RTX 4090/6000 Ada]
    
    TRAIN -->|Non, inference only| LATENCY{Latence critique?}
    LATENCY -->|Oui, <10ms| GROQ[Groq LPU]
    LATENCY -->|Non| CTX{Long context?}
    CTX -->|Oui, 128k+| H200[H200 / MI300X]
    CTX -->|Non| COST{Budget?}
    COST -->|Cloud| TPU[TPU v5e / H100 on-demand]
    COST -->|On-prem| MI300[MI300X / RTX 5090]
    COST -->|Edge| NPU[Apple M4 / Jetson]
    
    style H100B200 fill:#76b900,color:#fff
    style GROQ fill:#f7901e,color:#fff
    style NPU fill:#6750a4,color:#fff
```

## Outils & frameworks hardware découverts automatiquement

<!-- TOOLS_HARDWARE_START -->
### [pytorch](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch)
Tensors and Dynamic neural networks in Python with strong GPU acceleration

⭐ 101.8k | `Python` | Licence: NOASSERTION | MAJ: 2026-07-11
**Tags** : `autograd`, `deep-learning`, `gpu`, `machine-learning`, `neural-network`, `numpy`, `python`, `tensor`

### [alacritty](https://github.com/alacritty/alacritty)
A cross-platform, OpenGL terminal emulator.

⭐ 64.9k | `Rust` | Licence: Apache-2.0 | MAJ: 2026-07-11
**Tags** : `bsd`, `gpu`, `linux`, `macos`, `opengl`, `rust`, `terminal`, `terminal-emulators`

### [stats](https://github.com/exelban/stats)
macOS system monitor in your menu bar

⭐ 40.4k | `Swift` | Licence: MIT | MAJ: 2026-07-11
**Tags** : `battery`, `bluetooth`, `clock`, `cpu`, `disk`, `fans`, `gpu`, `macos`

### [taichi](https://github.com/taichi-dev/taichi)
Productive, portable, and performant GPU programming in Python.

⭐ 28.3k | `C++` | Licence: Apache-2.0 | MAJ: 2026-07-11
**Tags** : `computer-graphics`, `differentiable-programming`, `gpu`, `gpu-programming`, `sparse-computation`, `taichi`

### [fastai](https://github.com/fastai/fastai)
The fastai deep learning library

⭐ 28.1k | `Jupyter Notebook` | Licence: Apache-2.0 | MAJ: 2026-07-11
**Tags** : `colab`, `deep-learning`, `fastai`, `gpu`, `machine-learning`, `notebooks`, `python`, `pytorch`

### [FanControl.Releases](https://github.com/Rem0o/FanControl.Releases)
This is the release repository for Fan Control, a highly customizable fan controlling software for Windows.

⭐ 20.2k | Licence: NOASSERTION | MAJ: 2026-07-11
**Tags** : `control`, `cpu`, `curves`, `fan`, `fancontrol`, `gpu`, `pwm`, `speed`

### [nvidia-docker](https://github.com/NVIDIA/nvidia-docker)
Build and run Docker containers leveraging NVIDIA GPUs

⭐ 17.6k | Licence: Apache-2.0 | MAJ: 2026-07-11
**Tags** : `cuda`, `docker`, `gpu`, `nvidia-docker`

### [wgpu](https://github.com/gfx-rs/wgpu)
A cross-platform, safe, pure-Rust graphics API.

⭐ 17.6k | `Rust` | Licence: Apache-2.0 | MAJ: 2026-07-11
**Tags** : `d3d12`, `gpu`, `hacktoberfest`, `metal`, `opengl`, `rust`, `vulkan`, `webgpu`

### [WebGL-Fluid-Simulation](https://github.com/PavelDoGreat/WebGL-Fluid-Simulation)
Play with fluids in your browser (works even on mobile)

⭐ 16.5k | `JavaScript` | Licence: MIT | MAJ: 2026-07-11
**Tags** : `fluid`, `gpu`, `navier-stokes`, `simulation`, `webgl`

### [gpu.js](https://github.com/gpujs/gpu.js)
GPU Accelerated JavaScript

⭐ 15.4k | `JavaScript` | Licence: MIT | MAJ: 2026-07-09
**Tags** : `glsl`, `gpgpu`, `gpu`, `javascript`, `math`, `nodejs`, `webgl`

### [neovide](https://github.com/neovide/neovide)
No Nonsense Neovim Client in Rust

⭐ 15.1k | `Rust` | Licence: MIT | MAJ: 2026-07-11
**Tags** : `gpu`, `neovim`, `neovim-guis`, `rust`, `skia`

### [deeplearning4j](https://github.com/deeplearning4j/deeplearning4j)
Suite of tools for deploying and training deep learning models using the JVM. Highlights include model import for keras, tensorflow, and onnx/pytorch, a modular and tiny c++ library for running mat...

⭐ 14.2k | `Java` | Licence: Apache-2.0 | MAJ: 2026-07-11
**Tags** : `artificial-intelligence`, `clojure`, `deeplearning`, `deeplearning4j`, `dl4j`, `gpu`, `hadoop`, `intellij`

### [g-helper](https://github.com/seerge/g-helper)
Lightweight Armoury Crate alternative for Asus laptops with nearly the same functionality. Works with ROG Zephyrus, Flow, TUF, Strix, Scar, ProArt, Vivobook, Zenbook, Expertbook, ROG Ally, and many...

⭐ 14.1k | `C#` | Licence: GPL-3.0 | MAJ: 2026-07-11
**Tags** : `ally`, `amd`, `armoury`, `asus`, `aura`, `cpu`, `fan`, `g-helper`

### [Open3D](https://github.com/isl-org/Open3D)
Open3D: A Modern Library for 3D Data Processing

⭐ 13.8k | `C++` | Licence: NOASSERTION | MAJ: 2026-07-11
**Tags** : `3d`, `3d-perception`, `arm`, `computer-graphics`, `cpp`, `cuda`, `gpu`, `gui`

<!-- TOOLS_HARDWARE_END -->
